docs.title: EEG NPSI Tutorial
docs.text:
Bozza

Tutto il sito è una bozza e con contenuti random, generati quasi totalmente da Claude

A comprehensive, multi-language tutorial for EEG data analysis, covering Time-Frequency Analysis, ERSP, Connectivity, and Source Analysis — with code available in Python, R, and Matlab.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This tutorial provides a step-by-step guide to analyzing EEG data using standard pipelines reproducible across three programming languages. Each analysis step is presented with parallel code in Python, R, and Matlab so you can follow along in whichever environment you prefer.

  • Quick Start

    Get up and running in minutes. Install dependencies and load the example dataset.

    Installation

  • Time-Frequency Analysis

    Compute and visualize time-frequency representations using wavelets and short-time Fourier transforms.

    Tutorial 02

  • Connectivity

    Estimate functional and effective connectivity between EEG channels and regions of interest.

    Tutorial 04

  • Source Analysis

    Reconstruct the cortical sources of scalp EEG signals using inverse methods.

    Tutorial 05

docs.title: Languages
docs.text:
All tutorial code is available in three languages. Use the tabs throughout the site to switch between them.

Python R Matlab 
# Example: load EEG data with MNE
import mne
raw = mne.io.read_raw_fif('data/sample_eeg.fif', preload=True)
raw.filter(1., 40.)
raw.plot()

# Example: load EEG data with eegUtils
library(eegUtils)
raw <- import_raw("data/sample_eeg.bdf")
raw <- eeg_filter(raw, low_freq = 1, high_freq = 40)
plot(raw)

% Example: load EEG data with EEGLAB
[ALLEEG EEG CURRENTSET] = eeglab;
EEG = pop_biosig('data/sample_eeg.bdf');
EEG = pop_eegfiltnew(EEG, 1, 40);
eegplot(EEG.data);

docs.title: How to Use This Tutorial
docs.text:
1. Start with the Installation page to set up your environment
  2. Download the example dataset
  3. Follow the tutorials in order, or jump directly to the analysis you need
  4. Each tutorial page shows the same analysis in all three languages using tabs

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use this tutorial in your work, please cite:

Author (2025). EEG NPSI Tutorial: A multi-language guide to EEG analysis.
GitHub: https://github.com/GITHUBNICKNAME/EEG_NPSI_tutorial

See the full citation page for details.

docs.title: 02 · Time-Frequency Analysis
docs.text: Time-frequency analysis allows you to examine how the spectral content of the EEG signal changes over time. In this tutorial we compute time-frequency representations (TFR) using Morlet wavelets and the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT).

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Make sure you have completed Tutorial 01 · Preprocessing and have the cleaned epochs available.

docs.title: 1. Load Preprocessed Epochs
docs.text:
Python R Matlab 
import mne
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load preprocessed epochs
epochs = mne.read_epochs('outputs/results/epochs_clean.fif', preload=True)
print(epochs)

library(eegUtils)
library(tidyverse)

# Load preprocessed epochs
epochs <- readRDS("outputs/results/epochs_clean.rds")
print(epochs)

% Load preprocessed epochs (FieldTrip format)
load('outputs/results/epochs_clean.mat', 'data');
disp(data);

docs.title: 2. Morlet Wavelet Transform
docs.text:
Python R Matlab 
from mne.time_frequency import tfr_morlet

# Define frequencies of interest
freqs = np.arange(4, 50, 1)  # 4–50 Hz
n_cycles = freqs / 2.        # bandwidth: half the frequency

# Compute TFR
power = tfr_morlet(
    epochs,
    freqs=freqs,
    n_cycles=n_cycles,
    return_itc=False,
    average=True
)

# Plot
power.plot(['Oz'], baseline=(-0.5, 0), mode='logratio', title='Wavelet TFR - Oz')

# Using eegUtils + custom wavelet function
# (see r/code/utils_tfr.R for helper functions)
source("code/utils_tfr.R")

tfr <- compute_morlet_tfr(
  epochs,
  freqs    = seq(4, 50, by = 1),
  n_cycles = seq(4, 50, by = 1) / 2
)

plot_tfr(tfr, channel = "Oz", baseline = c(-0.5, 0))

% FieldTrip wavelet analysis
cfg            = [];
cfg.method     = 'wavelet';
cfg.foi        = 4:1:50;     % frequencies of interest
cfg.width      = cfg.foi/2;  % number of cycles
cfg.output     = 'pow';
cfg.toi        = -0.5:0.05:1.5;
cfg.channel    = 'Oz';
cfg.keeptrials = 'no';

TFR = ft_freqanalysis(cfg, data);
ft_singleplotTFR([], TFR);

docs.title: 3. Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT)
4. Save Results
docs.text:
Python R Matlab 
power.save('outputs/results/tfr_morlet-tfr.h5', overwrite=True)
power.plot_topo(baseline=(-0.5, 0), mode='logratio')
plt.savefig('outputs/figures/tfr_topo.png', dpi=150)

saveRDS(tfr, "outputs/results/tfr_morlet.rds")
ggsave("outputs/figures/tfr_topo.png", plot_tfr_topo(tfr), dpi = 150)

save('outputs/results/tfr_morlet.mat', 'TFR');
saveas(gcf, 'outputs/figures/tfr_topo.png');

Next step

Continue to Tutorial 03 · ERSP to compute Event-Related Spectral Perturbation from the TFR results.
